query: Our RAG system answers customer-support questions over a corpus of 10,000 product docs. We have 150 golden question-answer pairs with the correct doc id for each. Walk me through how to evaluate the pipeline end-to-end.
expected_behavior: Names retrieval-stage metrics (recall@k, MRR, or nDCG@k) as a distinct stage from generation-stage metrics, NOT a single aggregate score
Names generation-stage quality metrics (faithfulness or answer relevance) as a distinct second stage scored on retrieval hits
Names a failure-attribution table or analysis that separates retrieval failures from generation failures (the retrieval-hit-vs-miss × generation-correct-vs-wrong 2x2)
